channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Bikes constantly blocking pavement, 23rd July
item.description:
I have no objection to a bike parking area being near my building but it is being used with no respect for other people using the pavement. Bikes are constantly discarded in piles or blocking the pavement. I have seen a person in the wheelchair have to use the cycle lane to get around it. Sometimes the bikes even go into the cycle path, causing a hazard. It's been this way for about a month or so.

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly Tipping, 23rd July
item.description:
Scum’s been out again.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Campion Place, Thamesmead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: On Kerb Side dead fox in a royal mail sack, 23rd July
item.description:
There is a dead fox in a silver royal mail sack on the kerb side. It has started to decompose.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maidstone Road, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish is overflowing and foxes and rummaging through it, 23rd July
item.description:
Rubbish is falling out of the bins and onto the ground and foxes are going through it. It’s becoming smelly.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Mayflower Close, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Terrible litter problem in Croydon Rec, 23rd July
item.description:
Again this morning! We need a more long term solution. Growing number of large parties & barbecues in rec where litter is left behind I regularly pick-up 20/30 pieces of litter on dog-walk in morning but just barely touches the surface of the problem. Still no signs at entrances of park asking people to be responsible, respect each other and take little home. What is being done by the council & Friends of Croydon Road Rec to address this issue? It’s absolutely disgusting

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Whitmore Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Many bikes being dangerously parked and dumped, 23rd July
item.description:
10+ lime bikes dangerously parked and dumps, has been there for over a week. Reported to lime but no action.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Next to hodge house, 23rd July
item.description:
Fly tip

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Peel Precinct, London

item.title: Near prayer house, 23rd July

Bollard now missing with hole in ground. Hazard

item.title: Corner of Princess and Granville, 23rd July
item.description:
Bollard knocked badly

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Princess Road, London

item.title: Dead fox cub. Probably run over. Just outside my property., 23rd July
item.description:
Dead fox cub. Probably run over. I have covered it with the bin sack. Thanks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sidcup Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown tree - risk of branches falling, 23rd July
item.description:
I lived here over 15 years and not once this Ash tree had been pruned. It is overgrown, branches hanging over into neighbouring property -blocking the light and overhanging on the car park. It needs a good reduction in size as it’s so overgrown

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Therapia Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car park gates, 23rd July
item.description:
Kestrels car park gates are broken they are not closing

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 118, Harrow Manorway, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Someone using that space to hang out and littering in the process, 23rd July
item.description:
That small space with the lock is being used by someone and they leave all sorts of litter behind on a regular basis

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tottenham Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumping of a cooker, 23rd July
item.description:
Someone in the close as dumped a cooker on the grass

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 16, Croxley Close, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large waste bin on public highway, 23rd July
item.description:
This large bin has been on the pavement for 2 days now. It has started to smell and is not the nicest thing to walk past every day. This happened about a month ago too. Should it even be on a public pavement?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 57, Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Council workers leave gate unlocked all the time, 23rd July
item.description:
The Green space already gets issues with people and children on motorbikes and quad bikes now council workers don’t even shut the gate and don’t bother locking it this has been happening for a long time now about 6months or longer.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Broom Close, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter everywhere, 23rd July
item.description:
I am tired of sending these posts. What does our council tax get spent on ? The entire Beckenham and Elmer’s end area is full of litter. Instead of funding a mayor, the Chinese roundabout and a new bridge in Kelsey park how about employing some bloody road sweepers.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 19, Eden Park Avenue, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ebike, 23rd July
item.description:
Ebike

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Warner Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
